---
title: "Network Pharmacology Analysis of Curcumin Against Rheumatoid Arthritis"
author: "Musfirah"
date: "07-07-2026"
output: html_document
---

## Abstract
This study employed network pharmacology to investigate molecular 
mechanisms of Curcumin against Rheumatoid Arthritis (RA).

## Methods
Curcumin targets collected from HERB and SwissTargetPrediction.
RA genes from published literature. STRING database for PPI network.
enrichR for GO/KEGG pathway ^\enrichment.

## Results

### Target Collection
- HERB targets: 307
- SwissTargetPrediction: 97
- Combined: 382
- Intersection with RA genes: 2

### Hub Genes
all_genes
CD2 IL2
  1   1

### Key Pathways
Top KEGG pathway: **Allograft Rejection** 

Top GO process: **Natural Killer Cell Activation (GO:0030101)**

## Conclusion
Curcumin targets rheumatoid Arthritis through CD2 and IL2 
hub genes, modulating allograft rejection and natural killer cell activation.
